steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Count the relevant faces, dimensions, or scale factor before setting up equations. Lengths scale by $k$, areas by $k^2$, and volumes by $k^3$; for boxes and prisms, write the needed face or volume formula directly.

steps.title: Set up the hemisphere volume formula
steps.content:
A hemisphere is half a sphere. The volume of a sphere is $V = \frac{4}{3}\pi r^3$. Therefore, the volume of a hemisphere is:
$$V = \frac{1}{2} \cdot \frac{4}{3}\pi r^3 = \frac{2}{3}\pi r^3$$

steps.title: Substitute radius and calculate the volume
